title: Algorithmic Active Measures - data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis

fields.vision: A published-work surface on data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis.
fields.mechanism: It frames poisoned data as an operation against the foundations of analytic judgment, then uses a structured simulation to examine how coherent fabrications can deform AI-supported assessments.
fields.built_components: Built materials include the full manuscript DOCX, an abstract, methods section, a 243-item shared intelligence dataset described in the manuscript, red-team/blue-team role architecture, China and Russia target datasets, detection-rate table, analytic-outcome discussion, model-specific failure-mode discussion, and counterintelligence implications. The document reports detection rates from 10 to 40 percent depending on dataset and team, with strategic-assessment distortion as the substantive outcome of concern.
fields.source_utilization: Primary source authority for this page is the Algorithmic Active Measures manuscript itself. The page uses the manuscript for claim limit, research design, findings summary, and method vocabulary. It uses intelligence-analysis and structured-analytic-method and source background as method and source background, separate from publication metadata. The current evidence record contains no DOI, journal page, acceptance letter, or official citation; therefore the page must not claim peer-reviewed publication until one of those records is found.
fields.plan: Next work is editorial and evidentiary: verify submission or publication status; attach a public citation if accepted or published; reconcile author order with the manuscript title page; separate empirical findings from doctrinal implications; add fully source-checked references for data poisoning, adversarial machine learning, active measures, and intelligence tradecraft; and, if public release is intended, prepare an abstract-only public copy that does not expose sensitive exercise materials.
fields.status: Manuscript exists and has been converted into a public portfolio room. Publication metadata is pending: no DOI, journal page, or acceptance record is found in the current evidence record. The public status is research manuscript with publication metadata pending.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: confirm journal, DOI, acceptance, or submission record; decide whether to include the manuscript as a downloadable work product; verify whether any course-exercise details require redaction; bind every quantitative finding to the table and methods section; add an APA 7 bibliography only after the cited reference list is checked against the manuscript.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Treat data poisoning not merely as a technical attack on model behavior, but as counterintelligence tradecraft aimed at the informational foundations of analytic judgment. The manuscript asks how poisoned data can invert or deform intelligence conclusions even when analysts use contemporary LLM tools and human review.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The manuscript works through an original structured simulation: red teams representing state-level adversaries seeded roughly five to seven percent of a 243-item Arctic intelligence dataset with coherent fabrications; blue teams representing U.S. intelligence agencies used three commercial LLMs and human-in-the-loop tradecraft to detect manipulation and write assessments.

source_derived_signals.signal: Full DOCX extraction completed on 2026-05-08 for public-room synthesis.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use publication, validation, or operational-readiness language unless the supporting work product is named and retained.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A published-work surface on data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It frames poisoned data as an operation against the foundations of analytic judgment, then uses a structured simulation to examine how coherent fabrications can deform AI-supported assessments.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It connects AI security to active-measures logic: the central issue is not only model error, but adversarial shaping of what analysts think the evidence says.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It connects AI security to active-measures logic: the central issue is not only model error, but adversarial shaping of what analysts think the evidence says.
